zh.Powis Castle, Montgomeryshire, Wales: 威爾士蒙格莫利夏爾波維斯 卡素
zh.Welshpool, Montgomeryshire, Wales: 英國威爾斯蒙格莫利夏爾維爾申普爾
zh.lifeSummary: 當Maud Mortimer於1315年出生在英國賀福爾維格莫時，她的父親Roger Mortimer 1st Earl of March28歲，母親Joan de Geneville29歲。她於1329年與John Cherleton 2nd Baron Cherleton of Powis在英國威爾斯蒙格莫利夏爾維爾申普爾結婚。他們至少是1個兒子和2個女兒的父母。她去世於1345年7月5日在英國賀福爾賀福德享年30歲。

zh-hans.Welshpool, Montgomeryshire, Wales: 英国威尔斯蒙格莫利夏爾維爾申普爾
zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Maud Mortimer于1315年出生在英国賀福爾維格莫时，她的父亲Roger Mortimer 1st Earl of March28岁，母亲Joan de Geneville29岁。她于1329年与John Cherleton 2nd Baron Cherleton of Powis在英国威尔斯蒙格莫利夏爾維爾申普爾结婚。他们至少是1个儿子和2个女儿的父母。她去世于1345年7月5日在英国賀福爾賀福德享年30岁。

nameMeanings.name: Mortimer
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English and Scottish (of Norman origin): habitational name from Mortemer in Seine-Maritime, France, so called from Old French mort(e) ‘dead’ + mer ‘sea’, probably referring to a stagnant pond or drained swamp.

Irish (of Norman origin): the Norman surname in sense 1 above was taken to Ireland from England in the medieval period, where it has also been adopted by bearers of the Gaelic surnames Mac Muircheartaigh and ÓMuircheartaigh, commonly Anglicized as McMurty and Mortagh. Compare McMurdo .

variants.name: Mortimore
meanings: English (Devon; of Norman origin): variant of   Mortimer  .

variants.name: McMurdo
meanings: Scottish: Anglicized form of Gaelic Mac Muircheartaigh or Mac Muircheartaich, ‘son of Muircheartach’, a personal name meaning ‘sea-ruler’. In Scotland the name was often lenited to give McCurdy . Compare Murdoch and Moriarty . The name was also used to Anglicize Mac Murchaidh (see McMorrow ).

nameMeanings.name: Maud
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Medieval vernacular form of Matilda . This form was characteristically Low German (i.e. including medieval Dutch and Flemish). The wife of William the Conqueror , who bore this name, was the daughter of Baldwin , Count of Flanders. In Flemish and Dutch the letter -t- was generally lost when it occurred between vowels, giving forms such as Ma(h)auld. Maud or Matilda was also the name of the daughter ( 1102–67 ) of Henry I of England; she was married early in life to the Holy Roman Emperor Henry V , and later disputed the throne of England with her cousin Stephen . In 1128 she married Geoffrey , Count of Anjou. A medieval chronicler commented, ‘she was a good woman, but she had little bliss with him’. The name Maud became quite common in England in the 19th century, when its popularity was influenced in part by Tennyson's poem Maud, published in 1855 , but has not been much used since the early decades of the 20th century.

historicEvents.title: Famine Strikes Europe
historicEvents.description: A famine caused by cold winters and wet summers across Europe caused millions to die due to starvation. Desperation from hunger caused high levels of crime, stampedes in bread lines in London, infanticide, and even cannibalism throughout Europe.

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

historicEvents.title: The Hundred Years' War
historicEvents.description: The Hundred Years' War between France and England was largely due to the question of legitimate succession to the French crown.

en.lifeSummary: When Maud Mortimer was born in 1315, in Wigmore, Herefordshire, England, her father, Roger Mortimer 1st Earl of March, was 28 and her mother, Joan de Geneville, was 29. She married John Cherleton 2nd Baron Cherleton of Powis in 1329, in Welshpool, Montgomeryshire, Wales, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 1 son and 2 daughters. She died on 5 July 1345, in Hereford, Herefordshire, England, at the age of 30.

fr.Welshpool, Montgomeryshire, Wales: Welshpool, Montgomeryshire, Pays de Galles, Royaume-Uni
fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Maud Mortimer est née en 1315, à Wigmore, Herefordshire, Angleterre, son père, Roger Mortimer 1st Earl of March, avait 28 ans et sa mère, Joan de Geneville, avait 29 ans. Elle a épousé John Cherleton 2nd Baron Cherleton of Powis en 1329, à Welshpool, Montgomeryshire, Pays de Galles, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils et 2 filles. Elle est décédée le 5 juillet 1345, à Hereford, Herefordshire, Angleterre, à l’âge de 30 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Maud Mortimer 1315 in Wigmore, Herefordshire, England geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Roger Mortimer 1st Earl of March 28 und ihre Mutter Joan de Geneville 29. Sie hat 1329 in Welshpool, Montgomeryshire, Wales, United Kingdom John Cherleton 2nd Baron Cherleton of Powis geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn und 2 Töchter. Sie starb am 5. Juli 1345 im Alter von 30 Jahren in Hereford, Herefordshire, England.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Maud Mortimer è nata il nel 1315 nel Wigmore, Herefordshire, England, sua padre, Roger Mortimer 1st Earl of March, aveva 28 anni e sua madre, Joan de Geneville, aveva 29 anni. Lei sposato John Cherleton 2nd Baron Cherleton of Powis nel 1329, nel Welshpool, Montgomeryshire, Galles, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio e 2 figlie. Morì il 5 luglio 1345, nel Hereford, Herefordshire, England, all'età di 30 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Maud Mortimerが1315年にイギリスハーエフォードシュアイアウィグモーで生まれた時，父Roger Mortimer 1st Earl of Marchは28歳，母Joan de Genevilleは29歳であった。1329年にイギリスウェールズモントゴメリシュアイアウエルシュプオオルでJohn Cherleton 2nd Baron Cherleton of Powisと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子と2人の娘の親であった。彼女は1345年7月5日にイギリスハーエフォードシュアイアハーエフォードで30歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Maud Mortimer(이)가 1315년 영국 허퍼드실르 위그모어에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Roger Mortimer 1st Earl of March(은)는 28세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Joan de Geneville(은)는 29세였다. 그녀는 1329년 영국 웨일스 몽고머이실르 웰쉬포에서 John Cherleton 2nd Baron Cherleton of Powis(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1345년 7월 5일 영국 허퍼드실르 허퍼드에서 30세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Maud Mortimer nasceu em 1315, em Wigmore, Herefordshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, Roger Mortimer 1st Earl of March, tinha 28 anos e sua mãe, Joan de Geneville, tinha 29 anos. Ela casou-se com John Cherleton 2nd Baron Cherleton of Powis em 1329, em Welshpool, Montgomeryshire, Gales, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho e 2 filhas. Ela faleceu em 5 de julho de 1345, em Hereford, Herefordshire, Inglaterra, com 30 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Maud Mortimer родилась в 1315 г. в местности вигмор, хирефордшир, Англия, её отцу по имени Roger Mortimer 1st Earl of March было 28 лет, а матери, которую звали Joan de Geneville, 29. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени John Cherleton 2nd Baron Cherleton of Powis в 1329 г. в местности велшпул, монтгомиришир, Уэльс, Великобритания. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын и 2 дочери. Она умерла 5 июля 1345 г. в местности хирефорд, хирефордшир, Англия в возрасте 30 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Maud Mortimer nació en 1315, en Wigmore, Herefordshire, Inglaterra, su papá, Roger Mortimer 1st Earl of March, tenía 28 años y su mamá, Joan de Geneville, tenía 29 años. Se casó con John Cherleton 2nd Baron Cherleton of Powis en 1329, en Welshpool, Montgomeryshire, Gales, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hijo y 2 hijas. Murió el 5 de julio de 1345, en Hereford, Herefordshire, Inglaterra, a la edad de 30 años.
